claim: The start() guard short-circuits to a probe skip ONLY when initialReadyResult is truthy AND its .status is strictly equal to 'alive'. Missing .status, null, undefined, 'dead', or non-'alive' status all fall through to waitForDaemonReady().
counterevidence: None in production; only path for forging initialReadyResult is the test seam, which the bracketing tests cover.
alternativeExplanation: A truthy-only guard (initialReadyResult && initialReadyResult.status) would also short-circuit on {status:'dead'}; the existing session-proxy vitest at line 526-548 asserts probeCalls === 1 for that case, so this regression is pinned.
downgradeTrigger: if the guard short-circuited on truthy or on a non-strict status check this would flip to a P1.

claim: The reattach/cold-start call site does not pass initialReadyResult, so start() runs waitForDaemonReady(socketPath, probe, connect, log, { maxAttempts: maxColdStartAttempts }) independently. The launcher's only other createSessionProxy call site (the bridge path at line 323-332) does forward initialReadyResult: options.initialReadyResult, balancing producers and consumers.
counterevidence: None — grep found exactly two createSessionProxy call sites in the file.
alternativeExplanation: A future warm-detached hot-attach call site added without forwarding would silently regress; flagged as the downgrade trigger.
downgradeTrigger: if a third call site were added without forwarding initialReadyResult while it should this would need re-review.

claim: spawnSync is invoked with timeout: parsePositiveInteger(process.env.SPECKIT_PS_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS, 2000) at line 486; the guard at line 488 returns null on the first failure mode inspected (result.error), which Node sets with code 'ETIMEDOUT' when the timeout fires, BEFORE checking result.status or result.stdout. A defensive fallback (result.status !== 0) catches the edge case where a Node variant sets only result.signal on timeout-kill.
counterevidence: None — Node's contract documents spawnSync setting result.error on timeout. The result.status !== 0 branch is defense-in-depth for any Node quirk.
alternativeExplanation: If spawnSync were replaced with spawn+child.kill, the bounded-timeout contract would need re-verified.
downgradeTrigger: if spawnSync were swapped for a spawn+kill pattern or if the timeout option were inadvertently dropped this would need to be re-checked.

claim: All five new REQ-007 tests are non-vacuous: the bridge-probe test asserts the forwarded initialReadyResult matches {status:'alive'} (would fail if launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:489 dropped the forwarding); the three session-proxy tests assert concrete probe-call counts in a truth-table (alive→0, absent→1, dead→1) that one specific guard behavior regresses each one (no short-circuit, always-skip, truthy-check respectively).
counterevidence: None — each assertion removes one specific bit of behavior and would fail under that removal.
alternativeExplanation: None — the three-test status-truth-table is exactly the contract the spec demands.
downgradeTrigger: if a future change made start() accept a different status semantic (e.g. renames 'alive') the three tests would need updating in lockstep.

evidence: spec.md:110 compared to bridge :471-491, session-proxy :847-849, launcher :323-332, :483-487, :1909-1915.
checklist.md:176 (CHK-070 [P0]) and checklist.md:178 (CHK-071 [P1]) both ticked; cited evidence genuinely exercises the warm-owner skip and the bounded ps timeout.

no agent-definition change in REQ-007 scope
